questions.q: What is the primary driving force behind globalisation?
Discuss the impact of globalisation on the Indian economy.
questions.answer: Globalisation has led to increased foreign investment, improved technology, and access to global markets in India. However, it has also led to job losses, increased income inequality, and cultural homogenisation.

questions.q: Which of the following is a consequence of globalisation? (a) Increased national sovereignty (b) Decreased economic interdependence (c) Increased cultural diversity (d) Increased economic interdependence
What is the role of MNCs in the process of globalisation?
questions.answer: MNCs play a significant role in globalisation by investing in different countries, creating jobs, and transferring technology. They also contribute to the growth of international trade and the global economy.

questions.q: True or False: Globalisation has led to the decline of local industries.
Explain the concept of globalisation and its benefits.
questions.answer: Globalisation refers to the integration of economies and societies across the globe. Its benefits include increased economic growth, improved standard of living, and access to new technologies and markets.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______ sector has been most affected by globalisation in India.
What are the negative consequences of globalisation on the environment?
questions.answer: The negative consequences of globalisation on the environment include increased pollution, depletion of natural resources, and loss of biodiversity.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a feature of globalisation? (a) Increased economic interdependence (b) Cultural homogenisation (c) Decreased foreign investment (d) Improved technology
Discuss the impact of globalisation on Indian culture.
questions.answer: Globalisation has led to the spread of Western culture in India, resulting in cultural homogenisation. However, it has also led to the revival of traditional Indian culture and the promotion of cultural diversity.
